title: “Early stages of a very long war?”
summary: Admiral James Stavridis joins Scott Galloway and Jessica Tarlov to discuss potential outcomes for the current conflict in Iran, on Raging Moderates.
transcript_html:
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/4HeSv65TbJg

Transcript

Admiral, from a military standpoint, is there a clear offramp here, or are we looking at the very early stages of a longer war in the Middle East? >> I'll give you three scenarios very quickly. First one is the happy scenario. This would be where the people of Iran rise up and overthrow the revolutionary guard. I think sadly that's a 30% chance at best. The other 70% breaks into two scenarios. one, you could kind of think of it as Iran 3.0 that includes some remnants of this

government, but uh that behaves in a more moderate, sensible fashion. And then there's kind of the dark end of the spectrum in that 70% tunch, and that's that team moola Ayatollah toughs it out, takes the bombing, no boots on the ground are coming, and they repress the populace even worse. So there's a snapshot of the future as I see it.
